wall_line_handles.reason: paired counter-edge double-line, not wall thickness
paired cabinet-side vertical at panel gap

notes: Kitchen counter/sink unit on KIT layer with concentric circles and 20mm double-lines.

rationale.evidence: 307 of 308 entities sit on $0$KIT; three concentric CIRCLEs (r=81/91/95) plus 40 ARCs read as sink or cooktop detail. LINE pairs 3283/3284 and 3291/3292 are ~870mm horizontals only 20mm apart, and 328B/328C are a matching 20mm vertical pair—panel/counter thickness, not 100–200mm wall poche.
rationale.rule: Entities confined to a kitchen/furniture layer whose parallel pairs sit at ~20mm panel spacing are furniture carcass edges, not architectural walls.
rationale.counterfactual: If the same pairs were spaced 100–200mm on a WALL/벽 layer, or if room-scale wall poche replaced the concentric circles, this would become a plan fragment with real walls.
rationale.uncertainty: Whether the circles are a sink bowl versus a cooktop cannot be settled without labels or the insertion context.

wall_line_handles.reason: tall cabinet side at panel spacing

notes: Tall built-in cabinet/wardrobe on I-FL-FUR(B) with X panel marks.

rationale.evidence: 21/23 entities on I-FL-FUR(B) and 2 on F-FUR-FIX. Verticals 4136/4137 span ~1650mm at 20mm spacing; crossed diagonals 4146/4147 and 4149/414A are classic panel X marks. Aspect ~148×1650 matches a tall storage carcass.
rationale.rule: Built-in furniture layers with tall ~20mm-spaced carcass pairs plus diagonal X marks indicate cabinets, not partitions.
rationale.counterfactual: Absent furniture layers and X marks, a 1650mm double-line at 100–200mm spacing would read as a partition wall.
rationale.uncertainty: Appliance versus wardrobe contents behind the X panels cannot be told from vertex counts alone.

notes: Soft-furniture/fixture cluster (30 arcs) on I-FL-FUR(S).

rationale.evidence: All 125 entities on I-FL-FUR(S). Thirty ARCs and many short segments (31FF–320A) form curved fixture detail. Long lines 31FB and 3210 are unpaired boundaries, not wall-thickness doubles.
rationale.rule: A soft-furniture layer dominated by arcs and short segments with only isolated long boundaries is a furniture/fixture cluster, not a wall assembly.
rationale.counterfactual: Paired perimeter lines at wall spacing on a wall layer would flip this toward a room enclosure.
rationale.uncertainty: Exact item types (seating vs sanitary) cannot be identified from the histogram without rendering.

notes: Composite built-in unit mixing I-FL-FUR(B), I-FL-FUR(S), and F-FUR-FIX.

rationale.evidence: 93 entities across I-FL-FUR(B)=21, I-FL-FUR(S)=70, F-FUR-FIX=2. Verticals 2AE2/2AE3 are 1650mm at 20mm with X marks 2AF2/2AF3; S-layer from 2AF7 adds curved fixture detail. No 100–200mm wall pair.
rationale.rule: Coexistence of built-in carcass doubles and soft-fixture arcs on furniture layers marks composite furniture, not architecture.
rationale.counterfactual: If 2AE2/2AE3 were 100–200mm apart on a wall layer, they would indicate a real wall segment.
rationale.uncertainty: Which fixture the S-layer arcs represent cannot be resolved from this projection alone.

notes: Tall built-in cabinet run with shelf ladder and fixed-furniture caps.

rationale.evidence: 40/46 on I-FL-FUR(B), 6 on F-FUR-FIX. Verticals 33B7/33B8 ~1819mm at 20mm; 33C0 spans ~2344mm; horizontals 33BB–33BF form a shelf ladder; diagonals 33C4/33C5 and 33C7/33C8 are panel X marks.
rationale.rule: Regular shelf horizontals between ~20mm-spaced vertical carcass lines on a built-in layer denote storage cabinets, not walls.
rationale.counterfactual: Remove the shelf ladder and X marks and place the verticals on a wall layer at wall spacing, and it would read as a partition.
rationale.uncertainty: Open versus doored cabinet cannot be told from LWPOLYLINE counts alone.

wall_line_handles.reason: cabinet carcass side at panel gap

notes: Built-in cabinet carcass (1650mm) on I-FL-FUR(B)+F-FUR-FIX.

rationale.evidence: 28/32 on I-FL-FUR(B), 4 on F-FUR-FIX. Verticals 1E3F/1E40 are 1650mm at 20mm; 1E48 is a 1476mm inner edge; horizontals 1E43–1E47 plus X marks 1E4C/1E4D are shelf/panel detail.
rationale.rule: A ~20mm-spaced vertical pair enclosing short shelf horizontals on a built-in layer is a cabinet carcass, distinct from a wall.
rationale.counterfactual: Wall-layer placement with a 100mm+ gap would reclassify the pair as a wall fragment.
rationale.uncertainty: Door configuration and depth are not encoded in this 2D projection.

notes: Large soft-furniture/fixture cluster (48 arcs) on I-FL-FUR(S).

rationale.evidence: All 188 entities on I-FL-FUR(S) with 48 ARCs and dense short segments (4208–4221) forming repeated curved fittings. Bounding lines 4204 and 4206 are single edges; no wall-spaced parallel pair appears.
rationale.rule: High arc count plus dense small segments on a soft-furniture layer signals a fixture/seating cluster; lone perimeter lines are extents, not walls.
rationale.counterfactual: Paired perimeter lines at wall spacing on a wall layer would reclassify this as a room enclosure.
rationale.uncertainty: Fixture count and type inside the cluster cannot be enumerated from the histogram alone.

notes: Long shallow kitchen counter/base-cabinet run on FUR-FIX/FUR-KIT.

rationale.evidence: 21 entities on FUR-FIX and 3 on FUR-KIT; bbox ~1030×152 is a long shallow strip. Horizontal 3423 (~900mm) with short end verticals 3421/3422 (~150mm) and diagonal ticks 3419/341B mark a counter run, not a wall.
rationale.rule: A long shallow strip on kitchen-furniture layers with one long top edge and short end verticals is a counter/base-cabinet run.
rationale.counterfactual: A wall-layer paired inner line 100mm+ away would flip this to a wall.
rationale.uncertainty: Which appliances sit in the run cannot be derived from the sampled entities.

notes: Kitchen counter run (mirror family of def 8) on FUR-KIT/FUR-FIX.

rationale.evidence: 20 on FUR-KIT and 4 on FUR-FIX; bbox ~920×152 shallow strip. Horizontal 4F89 (~800mm) with end verticals 4F87/4F88 and ticks 4F7F/4F81 mirrors the counter geometry of definition 8.
rationale.rule: A shallow kitchen-furniture strip with one long top edge between short end verticals denotes a base-cabinet/counter run, not architecture.
rationale.counterfactual: Wall-layer placement with a wall-thickness paired line would flip this to a wall.
rationale.uncertainty: Sink or hob placement within the run is not confirmed in the sample.

notes: Curved decorative furniture object (splines/ellipses/arcs) on FUR.

rationale.evidence: All 107 entities on FUR: 45 SPLINEs, 31 ARCs, 15 ELLIPSEs, only 16 LINEs. Bbox ~49×538 with non-zero Z. The long LINE 6C5D is a center spine of a curved object, not an orthogonal wall face.
rationale.rule: Furniture-layer blocks dominated by splines and ellipses are organic/decorative objects and contain no wall geometry.
rationale.counterfactual: Orthogonal wall-thickness line pairs on a structural layer would be required to raise wall likelihood.
rationale.uncertainty: Exact object (planter, faucet, ornament) remains ambiguous from counts alone.

wall_line_handles.reason: short built-in carcass stub on FUR(B)

notes: Large composite furniture unit dominated by I-FL-FUR(S) arcs.

rationale.evidence: 127 entities: I-FL-FUR(S)=104, I-FL-FUR(B)=21, F-FUR-FIX=2, with 24 ARCs. Vertical 1E64 (~1490mm) is a single perimeter; S-layer detail from 1E65 onward is curved fixtures. No wall-spaced parallel pair.
rationale.rule: Mixed built-in and soft furniture layers with an arc-rich soft majority mark a composite furniture/fixture unit, not a wall.
rationale.counterfactual: Replacing 1E64 with a wall-thickness parallel pair on a wall layer would indicate a wall.
rationale.uncertainty: Fixture type driving the 24 arcs is not resolvable from the projection.

notes: Tall built-in cabinet with shelf ladder on I-FL-FUR(B)+F-FUR-FIX.

rationale.evidence: 28 on I-FL-FUR(B), 4 on F-FUR-FIX. Verticals 31D6 (~1940mm) and 31DE (~1766mm) bound a tall carcass; horizontals 31DA–31DC are shelves; X marks 31E2/31E3 and 31E5/31E6 confirm panels.
rationale.rule: A tall carcass with shelf ladder and diagonal panel marks on a built-in layer is a wardrobe/pantry cabinet, not a wall.
rationale.counterfactual: Move bounding verticals to a wall layer at wall spacing and drop shelves, and it would read as a partition.
rationale.uncertainty: Door/drawer count behind panel marks is not in the histogram.

wall_line_handles.reason: door-leaf twin line, not wall thickness

notes: Door leaf + swing arc symbol on DOOR layer (~920mm leaf).

rationale.evidence: All 14 entities on DOOR: LINEs 8251/8252 are ~920mm verticals spaced only 3mm (drawn leaf), ARC 8248 is the swing, and 11 LWPOLYLINEs form leaf/hardware. Bbox ~3×920 is door-width scale.
rationale.rule: A DOOR-layer block of a thin leaf pair plus swing arc is a reusable architectural door symbol, not furniture or a multi-element plan fragment.
rationale.counterfactual: Adjacent wall-thickness reveals or poche on a wall layer would push it toward 평면 부분도; furniture layers would push toward 가구.
rationale.uncertainty: Hand/swing direction and single versus paired leaf are not fully fixed from the sampled entities.

wall_line_handles.reason: cabinet carcass side at panel gap

notes: Built-in cabinet unit (1650mm carcass) same family as defs 5/6/12.

rationale.evidence: 28 on I-FL-FUR(B), 4 on F-FUR-FIX. Verticals 1EE8/1EE9 are 1650mm at 20mm; 1EF1 is a 1476mm inner edge; horizontals 1EED–1EEF and X marks 1EF5/1EF6 are shelf/panel detail.
rationale.rule: A ~20mm-spaced vertical carcass pair with shelf ladder and X marks on a built-in layer is a cabinet, not a wall.
rationale.counterfactual: Wall-layer placement with a 100–200mm gap would reclassify the pair as a wall.
rationale.uncertainty: Cabinet contents and door layout are not derivable here.

wall_line_handles.reason: 800mm vertical edge of tall fixed unit

notes: Tall narrow fixed furniture unit on FUR-FIX/FUR-KIT (~930mm).

rationale.evidence: 21 on FUR-FIX and 3 on FUR-KIT; bbox ~152×930 is tall and narrow. Vertical 41FD (~800mm) with short cross-lines 41EA/41FB/41FC and ticks 41F3/41F5 match a slim fixed cabinet (rotated relative to defs 8/9).
rationale.rule: A slim tall block on kitchen/fixed-furniture layers with one long edge and short cross-lines is a narrow fixed cabinet, not a wall.
rationale.counterfactual: A wall-layer paired line at wall thickness would flip it to a wall.
rationale.uncertainty: Pantry versus fridge enclosure versus utility cabinet cannot be distinguished from the histogram.

notes: Pipe/drain symbol: concentric circles with crosshair on PIPE layer.

rationale.evidence: Nine entities all on PIPE: CIRCLEs r=37.5/62.5/45, crossed LINEs 1E32/1E33 through the center, plus short pair 1E37/1E38. Geometry is schematic plumbing, not a carcass or wall.
rationale.rule: A PIPE-layer block of concentric circles with a centered crosshair is a plumbing/drain symbol, not scaled furniture or wall geometry.
rationale.counterfactual: A rectangular carcass would push toward furniture; wall-thickness pairs on a wall layer would make it a wall.
rationale.uncertainty: Floor drain versus riser versus cleanout cannot be settled without plotting style or a label.

notes: Ornate decorative furniture (362 splines, micro-segments) on FUR.

rationale.evidence: 865 entities all on FUR: 362 SPLINEs, 109 ARCs, 18 ELLIPSEs, 364 LINEs that are sub-millimeter (e.g. 6528 spans ~0.1mm). This micro-segment-plus-spline signature is ornate furniture, not orthogonal construction.
rationale.rule: Furniture-layer blocks whose lines are tiny segments interleaved with hundreds of splines are decorative objects and contain no wall lines.
rationale.counterfactual: Long orthogonal wall-thickness pairs on a structural layer would be required to indicate walls; none exist.
rationale.uncertainty: Specific piece (sofa, bed, planter) is not identifiable from entity counts without rendering.

notes: Small curved furniture/fixture object (166 splines) on FUR.

rationale.evidence: 402 entities all on FUR: 166 SPLINEs, 103 ARCs, 110 short LINEs; bbox ~167×78. Sampled lines 6969–6985 are sub-unit curve approximations, not straight walls.
rationale.rule: Dominance of splines and arcs with only short line segments on a furniture layer marks a curved decorative/appliance object, not a wall.
rationale.counterfactual: Long orthogonal wall-thickness pairs on a wall layer would flip the judgment.
rationale.uncertainty: Exact object (faucet set, fitting, small appliance) cannot be pinned down from the projection alone.

notes: Soft-furniture layout with long baseline and arc fittings on I-FL-FUR(S).

rationale.evidence: All 146 entities on I-FL-FUR(S) with 36 ARCs. Lines 5650 (~1890mm) and 5651 (~1010mm) are unpaired horizontals; short segments/arcs 5655–5668 form repeated fittings. No 100–200mm wall pair.
rationale.rule: A soft-furniture layer with isolated long baselines carrying arc-rich fitting detail is a furniture layout, not a wall enclosure.
rationale.counterfactual: If the baselines were paired at wall thickness on a wall layer, the block would read as a room boundary.
rationale.uncertainty: Which furniture set the repeated arc fittings represent is not identifiable from counts alone.

notes: Soft-furniture layout with 1270mm baseline on I-FL-FUR(S).

rationale.evidence: All 136 entities on I-FL-FUR(S) with 35 ARCs. Line 42E9 is a ~1270mm baseline; 42EA/42FC are ~400mm verticals; short segments/arcs 42EB–4306 form fittings similar to definition 19. No wall-spaced parallel pair.
rationale.rule: Isolated long baselines plus arc-rich fitting clusters on a soft-furniture layer denote a furniture layout rather than architecture.
rationale.counterfactual: Wall-thickness paired baselines on a wall layer would reclassify it as a wall enclosure.
rationale.uncertainty: Specific fittings within the layout cannot be enumerated from the entity histogram.
